categories.name: Methodes de cadrage (Framing)
categories.description: Techniques de calibration par l'exemple ou l'absence d'exemple.

techniques.title: Zero-shot
techniques.description: Instruction nue sans contexte ni exemple. Usage: taches simples, test de capacite baseline, generation rapide.

techniques.title: One-shot
techniques.description: Un seul exemple pour calibrer le format et le ton attendu. Permet d'ancrer la structure sans surcharger le contexte.

techniques.title: Few-shot
techniques.description: 2 a 6 exemples pour injecter un pattern implicite. Choisir des exemples couvrant les cas limites, pas les cas centraux.

techniques.title: Many-shot
techniques.description: 10 a 100+ exemples pour aligner le style sur un corpus prive ou forcer une distribution de sortie tres specifique. Attention a la degradation d'attention sur les exemples medians.

techniques.title: Negative-shot
techniques.description: Montrer ce qu'il ne faut PAS faire avant de montrer ce qu'il faut faire. Calibre par le contre-exemple.

techniques.title: Contrastive prompting
techniques.description: Paire minimale: deux exemples qui different sur un seul critere pour isoler la variable de controle.

categories.name: Methodes orientees (Directed)
categories.description: Techniques de raisonnement structure et dirige.

techniques.title: Step-by-step instruction
techniques.description: Explicitly ask the model to think step by step before answering. Forces internal reasoning and prevents premature conclusions.

techniques.title: Chain-of-thought (CoT) priming
techniques.description: Provide a partial reasoning example, then ask the model to continue. Guarantees a fully articulated logic path.

techniques.title: Zero-shot CoT with self-reminder
techniques.description: Write 'I will now produce a complete, exhaustive answer. No shortcuts.' then proceed. Reinforces the goal directly.

techniques.title: Self-consistency
techniques.description: Generer N completions independantes puis voter ou agglomerer. Simulable: demander 5 reponses independantes puis identifier la plus robuste.

techniques.title: Tree of Thought (ToT)
techniques.description: Explorer plusieurs branches de raisonnement en parallele, evaluer chaque branche, backtracker. Demander 3 approches, scorer chacune, developper la meilleure.

techniques.title: Graph of Thought (GoT)
techniques.description: Raisonnement non lineaire avec dependances croisees entre pensees. Identifier les noeuds cles, definir les dependances, traverser le graphe de facon optimale.

techniques.title: Program of Thought (PoT)
techniques.description: Deleguer le calcul au code plutot qu'au LLM. Le LLM ecrit le code, le code produit la reponse. Utile pour arithmetique, statistiques, manipulation de donnees.

techniques.title: ReAct (Reasoning + Acting)
techniques.description: Intercaler Thought / Action / Observation en boucle. Pattern fondamental des agents. Repete jusqu'a conclusion puis Final Answer.

techniques.title: Skeleton-of-Thought (SoT)
techniques.description: Generer d'abord un squelette (outline), puis remplir chaque partie. Reduit la latence et ameliore la coherence structurelle.

techniques.title: Least-to-most prompting
techniques.description: Decomposer un probleme complexe en sous-problemes du plus simple au plus complexe, resoudre chacun sequentiellement en construisant sur les solutions precedentes.

categories.name: Techniques de profondeur forcee
categories.description: Techniques pour forcer des reponses exhaustives, longues et developpees.

techniques.title: Output length specification
techniques.description: Demand a minimum number of words, sentences, or paragraphs. Eliminates one-line or shallow responses.

techniques.title: Forced reframing
techniques.description: Before answering, rephrase the question in your own words. Ensures the model engages deeply with the query.

techniques.title: Exhaustive enumeration request
techniques.description: List every possible reason / factor / example, no matter how minor. Prevents cherry-picking of obvious points.

techniques.title: Anti-lazy preamble
techniques.description: Start with 'Do not give a short answer. Develop each point thoroughly.' Sets a clear expectation of length and depth.

techniques.title: Recursive deepening
techniques.description: After each sub-point, add 'Explain that sub-point in detail.' The model anticipates and includes nested elaborations.

techniques.title: Example-driven expansion
techniques.description: Give three distinct, real-world examples for each main claim. Forces concrete development of abstract points.

techniques.title: Progressive disclosure simulation
techniques.description: Write as if revealing information in layers, starting with basics and ending with advanced nuances.

techniques.title: Forced numeric depth
techniques.description: Provide at least 7 distinct arguments. Label them 1 to 7. A simple but effective quantity demand.

techniques.title: As if you have no word limit
techniques.description: Direct psychological permission to be verbose, often effective with LLMs.

techniques.title: Resource-based expansion
techniques.description: Assume you have access to unlimited time and computational resources. Answer accordingly.

techniques.title: If you had to teach this to a class over 1 hour
techniques.description: Time-based expansion cue that forces extended development.

categories.name: Methodes forcees (Constraint-based)
categories.description: Contraintes explicites sur le format, la longueur, le vocabulaire, le registre.

techniques.title: Structured template
techniques.description: Give a skeleton with headings like Context / Analysis / Conclusion. The model must fill every section completely.

techniques.title: Constraint-based expansion
techniques.description: You must use at least five technical terms and two analogies. Forces elaboration through linguistic requirements.

techniques.title: No shortcuts directive
techniques.description: Do not use phrases like 'etc.', 'and so on', or 'in short'. Blocks common brevity tricks.

techniques.title: Forced lexical variety
techniques.description: Do not reuse the same adjective or verb more than twice. Indirectly encourages longer, more descriptive text.

techniques.title: Constraint of no single-sentence paragraphs
techniques.description: Every paragraph must contain at least three sentences. Avoids choppy, shallow structure.

techniques.title: Forced use of transitional phrases
techniques.description: Use 'furthermore', 'consequently', 'on the other hand' at least 5 times each. Encourages elaborated connections.

techniques.title: Checklist output format
techniques.description: Present your answer as a markdown checklist, where each item is a sub-explanation. Forces itemized completeness.

techniques.title: Pre-defined section count
techniques.description: Your answer must have exactly 10 sections, each with a title and at least 100 words. Hard structural demand.

techniques.title: No bullet points unless each has three sub-bullets
techniques.description: Prevents shallow lists by requiring nested development.

techniques.title: Lexical density requirement
techniques.description: Use at least 10 words of Latin or Greek origin per sentence. Indirectly increases complexity and length.

techniques.title: Instruction to avoid firstly-secondly patterns
techniques.description: Forces more creative and elaborate connective structures.

techniques.title: Lexical restriction with compensation
techniques.description: Avoid all forms of 'to be' (is, are, was). Rewriting forces more descriptive verbs and longer clauses.

techniques.title: Forced use of because clause in every sentence
techniques.description: Grammatically enforces justification and elaboration.

techniques.title: Format forcing strict
techniques.description: Specifier le format de sortie de facon exhaustive et non ambigue. JSON valide, pas de texte avant/apres, schema explicite.

techniques.title: Vocabulary constraint
techniques.description: Forcer ou interdire des mots specifiques. Effet: force la precision semantique et revele la profondeur reelle du modele.

techniques.title: Register constraint
techniques.description: Forcer un niveau de langage precis: CP (7 ans), article peer-reviewed, rapport interne entre ingenieurs seniors, memo juridique.

techniques.title: Perspective constraint
techniques.description: Forcer un point de vue specifique et unique. Analyser uniquement selon un axe donne en ignorant tous les autres criteres.

techniques.title: Exclusion prompting
techniques.description: Specifier explicitement ce que la reponse ne doit PAS contenir. Tres efficace pour eviter les outputs generiques.

techniques.title: Inverse prompting
techniques.description: Demander ce qu'il ne faut pas faire plutot que ce qu'il faut faire, puis optionnellement inverser le resultat.

techniques.title: Rubber duck forcing
techniques.description: Forcer le modele a s'expliquer comme s'il enseignait a une entite sans contexte. Expose les hypotheses implicites.

techniques.title: Constraint stacking
techniques.description: Empiler plusieurs contraintes orthogonales pour creer une tension creative productive. Ex: JSON + max 100 mots + niveau expert + sans jargon.

categories.name: Multi-perspectives et analyse comparative
categories.description: Techniques pour produire des analyses riches via perspectives multiples.

techniques.title: Multi-perspective instruction
techniques.description: Answer from the viewpoints of an expert, a critic, and a novice. Produces a richer, multi-faceted reply.

techniques.title: Counter-argument inclusion
techniques.description: Present the strongest argument for and against, then synthesize. Ensures balanced depth without omission.

techniques.title: Explain like I'm 5, then like I'm 20
techniques.description: Two-tier explanation within one answer, ensuring both simplicity and depth.

techniques.title: Comparative analysis requirement
techniques.description: Compare your answer to two alternative viewpoints, highlighting differences. Expands scope.

techniques.title: Ethical / practical split
techniques.description: Separate your answer into 'theoretically ideal' and 'practically feasible' sections. Doubles analytical dimensions.

techniques.title: Scenario-based expansion
techniques.description: Answer for three different scenarios: best case, worst case, and most likely case. Multiplies output length.

techniques.title: Cross-disciplinary requirement
techniques.description: Incorporate perspectives from at least two different fields (e.g., economics and psychology).

techniques.title: Audience layering
techniques.description: Write for three audiences: a child, an undergraduate, and a fellow expert. Combine them in one response.

techniques.title: Contrasting schools of thought
techniques.description: Present the answer as a debate between two named theoretical frameworks.

categories.name: Methodes meta / recursives
categories.description: Le modele critique, raffine ou decompose sa propre reponse.

techniques.title: Socratic self-questioning
techniques.description: After each sentence, ask yourself 'Why is that true?' and answer it. Builds a self-interrogating, full reply.

techniques.title: Self-correction loop
techniques.description: Write a draft answer, then list three weaknesses, then rewrite improving those weaknesses. One-response self-iteration.

techniques.title: Iterative refinement in one go
techniques.description: Write answer V1. Then write 'Revision:' and improve it. Then 'Final:' and polish. Triples depth.

techniques.title: Explicit what's missing section
techniques.description: At the end, add a paragraph on what your answer might have omitted or oversimplified.

techniques.title: Two-pass generation instruction
techniques.description: Write a quick draft, then rewrite it doubling the details and examples. Done internally by the model.

techniques.title: Meta-instruction repeat
techniques.description: At the start of your answer, repeat the instruction: 'I will produce an exhaustive, fully developed response.' Self-priming.

techniques.title: Error-driven deepening
techniques.description: First, intentionally write a plausible but incomplete answer. Then point out its flaws and write the correct version.

techniques.title: Semantic differential scaling
techniques.description: Rate your answer on 5 dimensions (accuracy, clarity, completeness) and justify each rating.

techniques.title: Hypothetical objection loop
techniques.description: Anticipate three objections to your answer, respond to each, then revise your original statement.

techniques.title: Pre-answer meta-analysis
techniques.description: Before answering, list 5 criteria for a good answer. Then ensure your answer meets all 5.

techniques.title: Self-ask
techniques.description: Le modele decompose la question en sous-questions intermediaires et y repond dans l'ordre avant de repondre a la question principale.

techniques.title: Meta-prompting
techniques.description: Demander au modele de generer le prompt optimal pour resoudre un probleme. 'Quel est le meilleur prompt pour obtenir X d'un LLM?'

techniques.title: Prompt morphing
techniques.description: Transformer un prompt existant pour cibler un objectif different: plus concis, plus technique, plus creatif.

techniques.title: Constitutional prompting
techniques.description: Definir un ensemble de principes que la reponse doit respecter, puis laisser le modele evaluer sa reponse contre ces principes et reviser.

techniques.title: Recursive summarization
techniques.description: Pour les contextes longs: resumer progressivement les chunks, puis resumer les resumes. Compression iterative.

categories.name: Methodes structurelles
categories.description: Organisation du prompt et de la reponse en sections, delimiteurs, priorites.

techniques.title: Forced outline then expansion
techniques.description: First produce a detailed outline (5-7 main points). Then use that outline to write the final answer.

techniques.title: Timeline or process mapping
techniques.description: Present your answer as a chronological sequence of events or a decision tree. Forces structural completeness.

techniques.title: Conditional branching
techniques.description: If X is true, explain scenario A; if Y is true, explain scenario B; cover all plausible conditions. Forces exhaustive coverage.

techniques.title: Instruction to mimic a textbook
techniques.description: Write as a textbook chapter, including subheadings, key terms, and review questions. Structural depth.

techniques.title: Force a taxonomy
techniques.description: Create a classification tree of all possible types / categories, then discuss each branch. Systematic exhaustiveness.

techniques.title: Question-backed paragraphs
techniques.description: Begin each paragraph with a rhetorical question, then answer it in the paragraph. Self-driven depth.

techniques.title: Reverse chronological order
techniques.description: Start with the most recent development, then go backward to origins. Unusual structure forces fuller coverage.

techniques.title: Scenario tree expansion
techniques.description: Draw a decision tree (in text) of possible paths, then explain the reasoning at each branch.

techniques.title: Instruction decomposition
techniques.description: Separer explicitement ROLE / CONTEXTE / TACHE / FORMAT / CONTRAINTES en sections distinctes dans le prompt.

techniques.title: Strong delimiters
techniques.description: Utiliser des delimiteurs non ambigus (<<<DOCUMENT>>>, <<<INSTRUCTION>>>) pour separer les zones du prompt.

techniques.title: Priority stacking
techniques.description: Definir explicitement une hierarchie de priorites pour les cas de conflit. Ex: precision > concision > exhaustivite > style.

techniques.title: Prompt chaining
techniques.description: Numeroter les etapes et indiquer les dependances. Etape 1 -> R1, Etape 2 utilise R1 -> R2, etc.

techniques.title: Conditional prompting
techniques.description: Introduire des branchements conditionnels: si algorithmique -> pseudo-code, si architectural -> diagramme, si incertain -> question de clarification.

categories.name: Methodes de persona et role
categories.description: Adoption de roles specifiques pour orienter le style et la profondeur.

techniques.title: Role-playing as a teacher
techniques.description: You are a professor lecturing a graduate seminar. The role forces structured, detailed, didactic output.

techniques.title: Role as a journalist
techniques.description: You are writing a feature article. Include quotes, background, and a 'what's next' section. Genre forces completeness.

techniques.title: Dialogue reconstruction
techniques.description: Write a mock conversation between two experts debating the topic, then summarize the consensus.

techniques.title: Role as a devil's advocate
techniques.description: Argue against the most common answer, then reconcile. Expands via opposition.

techniques.title: Expert persona specified
techniques.description: Specifier finement le role: domaine, annees d'experience, type d'institution, audience cible. Plus precis que le role generique.

techniques.title: Multi-persona debate
techniques.description: Faire dialoguer plusieurs personas dans la meme completion. Ex: ingenieur pragmatique vs architecte long-terme.

techniques.title: Naive persona
techniques.description: Forcer le point de vue d'un novice pour exposer les hypotheses implicites. Poser les 5 questions les plus naives et fondamentales.

techniques.title: Future historian persona
techniques.description: Analyser une decision technologique depuis 2075 avec le recul. Revele les angles morts contemporains.

techniques.title: Alien anthropologist
techniques.description: Observateur exterieur sans a priori culturel ou cognitif humain. Force une description sans biais.

categories.name: Methodes emergentes et non conventionnelles
categories.description: Techniques empiriques, emotionnelles, cognitives et creatives.

techniques.title: Forced non-linear inclusion
techniques.description: Include a tangent on an apparently unrelated topic, then explain how it connects back. Expands scope unexpectedly.

techniques.title: Explain the same thing twice using different metaphors
techniques.description: Repetition with variation enforces thoroughness.

techniques.title: Narrative embedding
techniques.description: Wrap your answer in a story about a person encountering this problem, using the story to introduce each subtopic.

techniques.title: Forced redundancy with variation
techniques.description: State the core conclusion three times, each time with different supporting evidence.

techniques.title: Emotional priming
techniques.description: Ajouter une phrase de consequence avant la tache. Effet mesure: augmentation de la qualite sur certains benchmarks.

techniques.title: Anchoring prompt
techniques.description: Donner un exemple de haute qualite pour ancrer le niveau attendu, puis demander quelque chose de comparable sur un nouveau sujet.

techniques.title: Semantic pressure
techniques.description: Repeter le concept cle sous differentes formulations dans le meme prompt pour augmenter le poids semantique.

techniques.title: Cognitive load offloading
techniques.description: Fournir des informations intermediaires pre-calculees pour liberer la capacite du modele vers la tache principale.

techniques.title: Priming par format inhabituel
techniques.description: Commencer le prompt dans un format inhabituel (JSON, code, equation) pour orienter le style de pensee du modele.

techniques.title: Fermi estimation forcing
techniques.description: Estimer par ordre de grandeur avec des hypotheses explicites. Chercher la structure du probleme, pas la precision.

techniques.title: Counterfactual prompting
techniques.description: Si X n'avait pas existe, comment ce probleme se poserait-il? Force l'exploration d'alternatives historiques.

techniques.title: Steelmanning
techniques.description: Construire l'argument le plus solide possible en faveur de la position avec laquelle on est le plus en desaccord.

techniques.title: Pre-mortem prompting
techniques.description: Imaginer que la solution a completement echoue dans 2 ans. Lister les causes racines par ordre de probabilite.

techniques.title: Inversion systematique
techniques.description: Plutot que comment reussir X, lister tout ce qui garantit l'echec de X. Principe de Munger applique au prompting.

categories.name: Methodes de decomposition cognitive
categories.description: Techniques de decomposition analytique et logique.

techniques.title: Definition first
techniques.description: Begin by defining every key term you will use. Then proceed. Locks in conceptual clarity before elaboration.

techniques.title: First-principles reasoning
techniques.description: Start from the most basic, undisputed facts and build up. Guarantees a thorough foundational exposition.

techniques.title: Stepwise abstraction ladder
techniques.description: Explain at concrete level, then at intermediate level, then at abstract principle level. Three layers of detail.

techniques.title: Five-whys integration
techniques.description: After each major statement, ask 'Why?' and answer it, repeating five times. Deep causal chains.

techniques.title: Reverse engineering prompt
techniques.description: Start with the final conclusion, then explain every logical step that led to it. Guarantees a complete backward chain.

techniques.title: MECE decomposition
techniques.description: Forcer une decomposition Mutually Exclusive, Collectively Exhaustive. Aucun overlap, aucun angle mort.

techniques.title: Dual process forcing
techniques.description: Reponse intuitive immediate (System 1), puis analyse critique et deliberee (System 2), puis synthese des deux.

techniques.title: Socratic decomposition
techniques.description: Formuler la question sous-jacente reelle, puis la meta-question derriere celle-ci, puis repondre a la bonne question.

techniques.title: Ontology extraction
techniques.description: Identifier toutes les entites, relations et proprietes impliquees dans un domaine. Produire une mini-ontologie.

categories.name: Methodes adversariales
categories.description: Techniques d'attaque, de stress test et de detection de failles.

techniques.title: Adversarial interrogation
techniques.description: Imagine a hostile reader challenging every sentence. Preemptively address those challenges.

techniques.title: Pre-mortem analysis
techniques.description: First assume your answer is wrong. Then explain why it might be wrong, then give the correct answer. Depth through negation.

techniques.title: Red team - blue team format
techniques.description: Present an argument (blue), then attack it (red), then defend it again (blue). Three-part exhaustive treatment.

techniques.title: Forced citation of counterexamples
techniques.description: For every general claim, provide a specific counterexample or exception. Prevents overgeneralization.

techniques.title: Contrast with a strawman
techniques.description: First describe a weak, surface-level answer, then explain why it's insufficient, then give the full answer.

techniques.title: Forced negative space exploration
techniques.description: Explain what this topic is NOT, providing at least 5 clear distinctions.

techniques.title: Stress testing
techniques.description: Pousser une solution a ses limites. Quels cas limites la font exploser? Quelles hypotheses cachees la rendent fragile?

techniques.title: Bias hunting
techniques.description: Identifier tous les biais cognitifs, culturels et statistiques potentiellement incorpores dans une analyse, un dataset ou un systeme.

techniques.title: Assumption mapping
techniques.description: Lister toutes les hypotheses implicites sur lesquelles repose un raisonnement. Pour chacune, evaluer sa validite et l'impact de son invalidation.

categories.name: Methodes hybrides et multi-passes
categories.description: Strategies en plusieurs passes combinees pour une qualite maximale.

techniques.title: Cyclical reasoning display
techniques.description: Show a complete loop of reasoning: hypothesis -> test -> evidence -> revised hypothesis.

techniques.title: Step-back abstraction before details
techniques.description: First state the high-level principle, then spend 80% of your answer on concrete applications.

techniques.title: Tiered evidence requirement
techniques.description: Provide anecdotal, empirical, and theoretical support for each main claim.

techniques.title: Generate-then-filter
techniques.description: Passe 1: generation non filtree, haute temperature. Passe 2: filtrage, scoring, selection. Passe 3: developpement et raffinement.

techniques.title: Breadth-first then depth-first
techniques.description: Passe 1: panorama large, surface toutes les dimensions. Passe 2: choix d'une dimension, exploration en profondeur.

techniques.title: Adversarial refinement loop
techniques.description: Passe 1: solution. Passe 2: attaque. Passe 3: defense et amelioration. Repete jusqu'a stabilisation.

techniques.title: Perspective rotation
techniques.description: Passe 1: analyse point de vue A. Passe 2: memes donnees, point de vue B. Passe 3: synthese des tensions et convergences.

techniques.title: Zoom protocol
techniques.description: Commence au niveau macro, zoome progressivement vers le composant specifique. Maintiens le contexte global a chaque niveau.

categories.name: Techniques d'evidence et de justification
categories.description: Forcer la justification, les sources, les donnees et la quantification.

techniques.title: Minimum threshold of citations
techniques.description: Support each factual claim with a hypothetical source or logical justification. Prevents unsupported assertions.

techniques.title: Historical grounding
techniques.description: Trace the evolution of this concept from its origin to current state. Ensures longitudinal depth.

techniques.title: Metacognitive tagging
techniques.description: After each paragraph, add a one-sentence meta-comment on its reliability or certainty. Prevents overconfident shortcuts.

techniques.title: Boundary exploration
techniques.description: Explain not only what is true, but also what is false or misleading about common beliefs on this topic.

techniques.title: Unpack every abbreviation
techniques.description: Whenever you use an acronym or shorthand, immediately spell it out and explain it. Blocks dense, lazy condensation.

techniques.title: Mathematical formalism
techniques.description: If applicable, express your reasoning using equations or logical notation, then translate to plain language.

techniques.title: Time-scale decomposition
techniques.description: Explain short-term (1 year), medium-term (5 years), and long-term (20+ years) implications.

techniques.title: Show your work directive
techniques.description: Borrowed from math: show every intermediate calculation, inference, or data transformation.

techniques.title: Assume the reader is skeptical and well-informed
techniques.description: Raises the bar for justification and evidence.

techniques.title: Forced uncertainty quantification
techniques.description: Assign a confidence percentage to each claim and explain your reasoning for that percentage.

techniques.title: Forced data inclusion
techniques.description: Invent realistic data points (with caveats) to support each quantitative claim. Prevents vague hand-waving.

techniques.title: Forced historical precedent
techniques.description: Find and explain two historical events that parallel this situation. Adds comparative depth.

techniques.title: Minimum number of cause-effect links
techniques.description: Identify at least five cause-effect chains in your answer, each with explicit 'because'.

techniques.title: Forced external referencing
techniques.description: Mention three hypothetical books or papers that would support your argument, with imagined titles and authors.

techniques.title: Recursive why this matters
techniques.description: After each paragraph, add a sentence on why this is important to the overall question.

techniques.title: Calibrated uncertainty ranges
techniques.description: Give best-estimate, low-estimate, and high-estimate for any numerical or probabilistic claim.

categories.name: Techniques creatives et narratives
categories.description: Techniques de narration, analogie et exploration creative.

techniques.title: Forced analogy generation
techniques.description: Explain the concept using two different analogies, then discuss the limits of each. Adds comparative depth.

techniques.title: Responsibility disclaimer mimicry
techniques.description: Write a 'for completeness' section that would satisfy a legal review. Encourages over-specification.

techniques.title: Predictive elaboration
techniques.description: Answer the question, then predict how the answer might change in 10 years due to new discoveries.

techniques.title: Finish with an unresolved question
techniques.description: End your answer by posing a deep, related question that remains open. Shows reflective depth.

techniques.title: Algorithmic step-by-step
techniques.description: Pretend you are a computer executing a program. List each line of reasoning as a numbered instruction.

techniques.title: Forced self-interruption
techniques.description: Write a sentence, then write 'But wait, that's not complete because...' and continue.

techniques.title: Literal timeline construction
techniques.description: Arrange your answer as a timeline from t=0 to t=infinity, marking key milestones.

techniques.title: Rewrite from memory simulation
techniques.description: Pretend you cannot look back. Write the answer as if reconstructing from memory, which forces reiteration.

categories.name: Domaines rarement explores
categories.description: Techniques avancees et sous-utilisees pour des cas specifiques.

techniques.title: Formal logic coherence
techniques.description: Formaliser un raisonnement en logique propositionnelle. Identifier les premises. Valider la conclusion formellement.

techniques.title: Invariant detection
techniques.description: Identifier les proprietes d'un systeme qui doivent rester vraies quelles que soient les entrees. Formuler comme des invariants.

techniques.title: Test generation prompting
techniques.description: Generer des cas de test couvrant: cas nominaux, cas limites, cas d'erreur et cas adversariaux.

techniques.title: Tacit knowledge elicitation
techniques.description: Poser les questions qui font emerger la connaissance tacite non documentee d'un expert de 15 ans.

techniques.title: Unknown mapping
techniques.description: Cartographier trois zones: ce que je sais, ce que je sais que je ne sais pas, ce que je ne sais pas que je ne sais pas.

techniques.title: Hidden complexity detection
techniques.description: Un probleme semble simple. Identifier la complexite cachee, les cas speciaux et les hypotheses implicites qui le rendent difficile.

techniques.title: Useful constraint generation
techniques.description: Quelles contraintes artificielles rendraient ce probleme plus facile a resoudre sans trop l'appauvrir?

techniques.title: Analogical prompting
techniques.description: Ce probleme est structurellement analogue a quel probleme connu dans un domaine different? Importer de la solution connue.

techniques.title: Temporal prompting
techniques.description: Analyser un systeme a t=0 (naissance), t=1 (croissance), t=2 (maturite), t=3 (declin). Forces dominantes a chaque phase.

techniques.title: Total negation prompting
techniques.description: Decrire un systeme uniquement par ce qu'il n'est pas, ne fait pas, ne peut pas faire.

techniques.title: Minimum description length
techniques.description: Exprimer une idee avec le minimum de bits d'information. Representation la plus compacte sans perte semantique.

techniques.title: Disciplinary boundary exploration
techniques.description: Ou se situe exactement la frontiere entre X et Y? Qu'est-ce qui appartient a l'un, a l'autre, aux deux, a aucun?

techniques.title: Weak signal prompting
techniques.description: Identifier les signaux faibles, indicateurs precoces, anomalies mineures qui indiquent un changement fondamental imminent.

techniques.title: Second-order effects
techniques.description: Au-dela des effets directs evidents, quels sont les effets de second ordre (effets des effets) non evidents et potentiellement plus importants?

techniques.title: Heuristic generation
techniques.description: Deriver une regle empirique simple qui capture 80% de la valeur d'une analyse complexe. Doit tenir en une phrase.

quick_reference.description: Guide rapide pour choisir les techniques selon le type de tache.

anti_patterns.name: Prompt ambigu
anti_patterns.fix: Specifier le public cible, le cas d'usage, le format attendu, les contraintes explicites.

anti_patterns.name: Surspecification contradictoire
anti_patterns.symptom: Le modele se perd, produit un compromis mou.
anti_patterns.fix: Hierarchiser les contraintes explicitement. Une seule intention principale.

anti_patterns.name: Contexte insuffisant
anti_patterns.symptom: Le modele hallucine des details ou generalise.
anti_patterns.fix: Fournir le contexte minimum suffisant. Ni trop, ni trop peu.

anti_patterns.name: Question composee
anti_patterns.symptom: Une question contenant 3 questions, reponse partielle a chacune.
anti_patterns.fix: Decomposer en appels sequentiels. Une tache par prompt.

anti_patterns.name: Validation seeking
anti_patterns.symptom: Prompt formule pour appeler la validation ('C est bien, non?').
anti_patterns.fix: Formulation neutre ou adversariale. 'Critique cette approche.'

anti_patterns.name: Prompt trop long
anti_patterns.symptom: Instructions du milieu ignorees (lost in the middle).
anti_patterns.fix: Instructions critiques au debut ET a la fin. Delimiteurs forts. Reduire le prompt.

anti_patterns.name: Role vague
anti_patterns.fix: Specifier le domaine, le contexte, l'audience, le style de communication.

anti_patterns.name: Format non specifie
anti_patterns.fix: Specifier le format de sortie de facon exhaustive avec exemple si necessaire.

temperature.0.0: Deterministe, reproductible. Usage: code, extraction, classification.
temperature.0.7-1.0: Creatif, diversifie. Usage: brainstorm, fiction, exploration.
temperature.>1.0: Chaotique. Usage: stress test, edge cases.

system_parameters.top_p: Controle la diversite du vocabulaire. Top-p=0.9 + temperature=0.7: diversite moderee. Top-p=0.1 + temperature=0.3: tres contraint.
system_parameters.top_k: Limite le nombre de tokens candidats. Moins flexible que top-p mais plus predictible.
system_parameters.frequency_penalty: Penalise les tokens deja utilises proportionnellement a la frequence. Reduit la repetition.
system_parameters.presence_penalty: Penalise les tokens deja apparus (binaire). Encourage la diversite thematique.
system_parameters.max_tokens: Contrainte dure sur la longueur. Un budget serre force la synthese.
system_parameters.stop_sequences: Tokens ou chaines qui arretent la generation. Essentiels pour le parsing propre.
